message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_diff F64 composes through narrow+sub for autograd

Same severed-autograd pattern Scatter / ScatterAdd / IndexPut had
until the recent fixes (5d4b5a1 / 56c5165 / 692d90e): tensor_diff's
F64 path extracted tensor_values per iteration and rebuilt a
requires_grad=false leaf, silently dropping the gradient that should
flow back to the input. PyTorch's torch.diff is fully differentiable.

The forward computes
    out[..., i] = x[..., i + 1] - x[..., i]
along the last dim, which is exactly
    narrow(x, last, 1, len-1) - narrow(x, last, 0, len-1).

Both narrow and sub already have backward kernels, so composing
through them gives the analytical telescoping derivative for free:
each x[..., i] for 0 < i < len-1 contributes +1 to the next-output
slot via the head-narrow and -1 to the previous-output slot via the
tail-narrow, canceling to 0 in the interior; the endpoints survive
as -1 (x[0]) and +1 (x[N-1]).

For n > 1 the diff is reapplied n times, producing the n-th discrete
difference operator. The composed graph still flows gradients through
every iteration.

The F32 path is left untouched — tape.narrow currently coerces to
F64, so composing F32 diff through it would silently change the
output dtype contract. F32 callers retain the existing
forward-only behaviour (consistent with the rest of the F32-only
codepaths in this module).

Tests:
  diff_n1_propagates_gradients_f64: length-5 input, sum loss,
    expect [-1, 0, 0, 0, +1] gradient (telescoped first difference).
  diff_n2_propagates_gradients_f64: length-5 input, sum loss with
    n=2, expect [+1, -1, 0, -1, +1] (second discrete difference
    coefficients).
  diff_propagates_gradients_along_last_dim_2d: [2, 4] input,
    confirms the gradient pattern replicates per row when diff
    operates only along the last dim.

Existing forward tests (diff_basic, diff_n2, diff_n0_is_identity,
diff_preserves_leading_dims_when_last_dim_lt_2,
diff_rejects_rank_zero_input) all continue to pass — bit-for-bit
forward parity preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
